briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now begin the climate briefing for April 3, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Intensification of Climate Change, Threats to Vulnerable Populations and Ecosystems
sections.content: News released today clearly demonstrates the impact of climate change on the most vulnerable segments of our society. Specifically, reports from both domestic and international sources indicate that children, who require 50% more air than adults, are most vulnerable to climate change and are directly impacted in terms of lung health [1, 4, 50]. Furthermore, climate change is having severe economic repercussions, including rising insurance premiums [2], surging lamb prices [52], and threats to Sicily's wine industry [44]. Concerns are also being raised about the potential collapse of modern agriculture, a drastic reduction in grazing lands [47, 48], and risks to coffee cultivation [59, 61]. The loss of natural ecosystems and cultural heritage is also severe, with Canadian muskoxen suffering from the dual burden of new diseases and climate change, and the permanent cancellation of dog sled races on Lake Minnetonka [42, 43].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea's Carbon Neutrality Implementation and Energy Transition
sections.content: Domestically, active efforts are underway to implement carbon neutrality and transition energy in response to climate change. The Seoul Metropolitan Government has presented a vision to create a climate-resilient, carbon-neutral city by transforming from an energy-consuming city to an energy-transition city [5]. POSCO Holdings has also emphasized the necessity of national and inter-industry cooperation to achieve carbon neutrality in the steel industry [7, 12]. Local governments like Gapyeong-gun are proactively responding to wildfires, heavy rains, and climate change to protect residents' safety [3]. Various regions, including Gwangmyeong-si, Chungnam Province, Gwangju, Gwangjin-gu, and Wonju-si, are encouraging citizen participation through initiatives such as expanding the Carbon Neutrality Point System, creating 'Carbon Neutral Forests,' and tree-planting campaigns [9, 10, 13, 14, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 24, 25]. Furthermore, efforts towards energy transition through technological development and investment are continuing, with Korea University uncovering key carbon neutrality technology mechanisms [22], Hyundai Motor Group emphasizing the importance of hydrogen energy [28], and Woori Financial Group making large-scale investments in offshore wind power [35]. Policy and social participation are also active, with the Ministry of Economy and Finance improving hidden regulations in the renewable energy sector [30], and 300 citizens participating in discussions on South Korea's carbon neutrality pathway [8, 26].

sections.title: 🌍 Global Climate Response and Policy Challenges
sections.content: The international community is engaged in diverse collaborations and efforts to address climate change, yet concerning policy shifts are also being observed simultaneously. Positive international cooperation continues, with KOICA and the French Development Agency Group discussing international development cooperation in climate change and infrastructure [6], Thailand and Sweden collaborating on climate change response [54], and California launching new strategies to protect its agriculture and food sectors [57]. In the UK, plug-in solar power is creating a new wave in energy transition, allowing tenants to also benefit from clean energy [37]. However, policy movements that run counter to climate change efforts are also being detected, such as the disappearance of climate change-related pages from Yellowstone National Park guidebooks [38], the planned closure of a US federal research institute conducting forest and climate change research [41], and anticipated multi-billion dollar cuts to clean energy and climate programs in Trump's 2027 budget proposal [46]. In India, strengthened regulations on renewable energy variability have raised concerns about investment contraction [29], and discussions continue regarding issues such as the cancellation of further North Sea oil drilling development [63].

sections.title: 📰 Social Perception and Scientific Insight on Climate Change
sections.content: Climate change is now influencing not just environmental issues but also broader societal perceptions, culture, and scientific insights. News that author Ania Hull is experiencing 'anticipatory grief' regarding the impacts of climate change [39] demonstrates how the climate crisis affects individual psychology, and efforts to hear citizens' voices are ongoing, such as community listening sessions held in Cook County [45]. The collaboration between 'Skins' actress Megan Prescott and Adam McKay for a climate change series [55] suggests that popular culture can become a new channel for conveying climate change messages. Meanwhile, the importance of a detailed scientific approach to climate change-related phenomena is highlighted, as seen in scientific analyses suggesting that the extended pollen allergy season is not due to climate change [56]. Discussions on the link between Google's artificial intelligence and carbon emissions [62] raise ethical questions in an era of technological advancement, and in-depth research into Earth's distant future continues, with scientists warning of brutal, planetary-scale climate change due to the emergence of a new supercontinent approximately 200 to 250 million years from now [51].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlights that as the threat of climate change intensifies, diverse response efforts, alongside policy and social challenges, coexist both domestically and internationally. This is a critical juncture where our sustained attention and action to overcome the climate crisis become even more crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing.
